council.name: The Strategist
council.role: Big picture, revenue opportunities, market positioning

scope: Coda ERP (DILESA, ANSA, ANSA-Ventas, SR Group, RDB)

memos.title: Inaugural Council Session
memos.summary: First session — analyzing current state and generating initial recommendations for BSOP, Coda ERP modernization, and the operating system behind Beto’s portfolio.

ideas.title: Stand up a live AI operations data layer
ideas.summary: Move BSOP AI dashboards from manually regenerated JSON snapshots toward a scheduled pipeline that syncs usage, costs, and agent activity into a live operational dataset.

ideas.title: Build an operations core for RDB around Waitry, not a replacement POS
ideas.summary: Keep Waitry where it works (QR ordering) and build the surrounding operations layer in Supabase/Coda for inventory, deduplication, and reconciliation instead of replacing the full stack prematurely.

ideas.title: Launch a Coda architecture tiger team for DILESA first
ideas.summary: Concentrate technical cleanup and migration work on the highest-leverage document first, using DILESA as the proving ground for schema rationalization and future external-db sync.

highlights.speaker: strategist
highlights.message: The platform is already becoming the executive cockpit. The next leverage move is not another view — it is making the data operationally alive so decisions happen from one system.

highlights.speaker: analyst
highlights.message: Static snapshots are good for v1 storytelling, but they break as an operating dashboard. Every stale metric erodes trust faster than a missing metric.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: Be careful not to start three migrations at once: BSOP live data, Coda cleanup, and RDB replacement. Pick one proving ground and sequence the rest.

debate.topic: Where should technical effort land first?

highlights.speaker: engineer
highlights.message: Use the AI Operations stack as the testbed for a sync pipeline. It has bounded scope, clear telemetry, and visible value. That pattern can later power travel, businesses, and RDB.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: RDB POS replacement is a trap right now. Wrap Waitry, dedupe aggressively, and build the back-office moat instead of chasing perfect front-of-house control.

highlights.speaker: strategist
highlights.message: Agree. The business win is reliability + visibility, not owning every transaction surface on day one.

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: Consensus: create one live-data spine, use DILESA and AI Operations as the two strategic proving grounds, and keep RDB focused on augmentation rather than replacement.

recommendations.title: Ship a live sync pipeline for AI Operations metrics
recommendations.rationale: It turns BSOP from a polished snapshot into a trusted operating console and establishes the data pattern for the rest of the platform.
actionItems: Define Supabase schema for usage, sessions, and agent runs
Build a sync script from OpenClaw JSONL/manifest outputs
Update BSOP AI pages to consume live or regenerated normalized data

recommendations.title: Treat DILESA as the first formal Coda modernization program
recommendations.rationale: DILESA has the highest table count and the largest downstream payoff if schema and dependencies are cleaned up deliberately.
actionItems: Identify top 10 god tables by risk and workflow centrality

recommendations.title: For RDB, build the ops core around Waitry instead of replacing it
recommendations.rationale: This reduces near-term risk, preserves what already works, and focuses engineering time on data integrity, inventory flow, and reconciliations.
actionItems: Scope the minimum viable Supabase back-office layer
